summary: Installed pkgs.gnomeExtensions.mouse-follows-focus-2 (dev-tools.nix) and enabled the verified UUID mouse-follows-focus@crisidev.org in gnome.nix enabled-extensions, preserving focus-mode=sloppy and focus-change-on-pointer-rest=false unchanged. nix flake check, home-manager build, and home-manager switch --flake .#benjamin all succeeded; live dconf confirmed. Phase 5 behavioral verification requires a GNOME Wayland re-login and manual testing by the user, and is reported as pending rather than fabricated. A shell-version mismatch (extension declares up to GNOME Shell 49, installed is 50.1) was flagged as an unresolved risk for the user to check post-re-login.

artifacts.summary: Enabled verified UUID mouse-follows-focus@crisidev.org in enabled-extensions; focus-mode/focus-change-on-pointer-rest left unchanged
Implementation summary with resolved UUID, build/switch verification, and outstanding manual re-login/behavioral-test step

completion_data.completion_summary: Installed and enabled the mouse-follows-focus GNOME Shell extension (UUID mouse-follows-focus@crisidev.org, verified from the built package's metadata.json) via home-manager, preserving focus-mode=sloppy; nix flake check, home-manager build, and home-manager switch all passed. Behavioral verification (Phase 5) requires a GNOME Wayland re-login and is pending user action.

next_steps: User must log out and log back in to the GNOME Wayland session for the extension to load, then run gnome-extensions info mouse-follows-focus@crisidev.org to confirm ENABLED state (watch for a shell-version-50-vs-declared-49 rejection), then walk through the plan's Phase 5 manual behavioral test checklist (hover-to-focus, keyboard-switch-sticks, no-snap-back).
